dialogos.texto_en: Futaro-kun, hi.
Oh, Futaro, you came here too.\nWhat a coincidence.
What are you guys talking about here?
We were discussing what we should see\nat Churaumi Aquarium later.
Hey, Futaro.\nLet's go around the aquarium together.
Wait, aren't we all going as a group?
Even if everyone's there,\nwe can still go around together.
W-well...\nI guess, that's true...
Hmmm?\nAlright, so what are you gonna do, Futaro-kun?
Nothing in particular.\nI'll just follow your guys' lead.
So?\nWhat are you guys looking forward to?
For me, it's definitely the big tank.\nYou can see big fish up close there.
Well, that's something you can't miss.\nIt's like the main exhibit at the aquarium.
How about you, Ichika?
I'm interested in the jellyfish.\nApparently, there are quite a few different types.
And, the tropical fish, maybe.\nPerhaps we'll see some fish that we couldn't see\nwhile snorkeling yesterday.
Will we see that other fish too?\nThe one with the name I'd never heard of...\n'gurukun?'
Oh, isn't that the name the locals use?\nIn the Okinawan language.
Yeah, probably.
The only other Okinawan word I know is 'irabu.'
That's a sea snake, right? I've heard of that too.
Speaking of which, I saw a sign for 'irabu soup'\nat a restaurant, and I wondered what it was...
I was pretty astonished by the rather graphic image\nthat popped up when I searched for it.
Yeah, all chopped up like that,\nI can imagine it would be quite shocking.
I've heard that snake tastes like chicken,\nbut what about irabu...?
Hm? Wait a minute.\nIs a sea snake a snake, or a fish?
It's not that simple. Even if it's called a sea snake,\nit could be a kind of fish, or a reptile.
What the...? How confusing.
Hey, don't complain to me.\nYou'll have to check the description at the aquarium\nto be sure.
There are quite a few things that look like snakes,\nlike freshwater eel, conger eel and moray eel, right?
Futaro-kun, do you have anything you're looking forward\nto seeing at the aquarium? Any fish you want to see?
I want to know that too.
Nothing in particular.
Whaaaa?\nDon't say things like that.\nTry to be a little more interested, okay?
Could it be that you're not interested in the aquarium?
No way.\nSince we've come all the way here,\nI'm going to get the most value out of this as possible.
Hahaha, what's that supposed to mean?\nThis isn't an all-you-can-eat restaurant,\nso I don't think that's the right way to put it.
I also want to see it with you, Futaro.
Wha...?\nWell, I mean, this is our graduation trip, so being\nwith you guys and Raiha is the best case, right?
Hmm...\nFutaro-kun, I don't think that's necessarily true.
It's gotta be the whale shark. I'm excited for that.
Yeah, yeah, I can understand.
I get that.\nI definitely plan on seeing the whale sharks myself.
Right?\nI mean, they're the largest fish in the world!
But aren't whales bigger?\nLike the blue whale or something.
Whales are mammals, aren't they?
Oh, right.\nAhaha...
The whale shark's pattern on its body\nis said to resemble a traditional\nJapanese summer garment called a Jinbei.
Then when we see it,\nlet's pay attention to its pattern too.
I also want to see it with you, Futaro.
Wha?\nWell, I mean, this is our graduation trip, so being\nwith you guys and Raiha is the best case, right?
Hmm...\nFutaro-kun, I don't think that's necessarily true.
Let's see.\nI've gotta go with the deep sea exhibit.
Deep sea?
Wow, you're taking an unexpected approach.
No no, it's just that\nthe deep sea is known as the last frontier for humanity,\nbecause we haven't been able to investigate it very much.
Don't you feel a sense of romance\nin being able to see creatures\nthat come from such a place?
Boys like that kind of thing, don't they?
Can creatures even live in the deep sea?\nThey'd probably get flattened.
The deep sea refers to depths deeper than 200 meters.\nThere are plenty of deep sea fish\nthat live in shallower waters.
Deep sea fish have such unique characteristics, huh.\nLike having large eyes, or a strange shape.
There are even those with transparent heads.\nLooking at them is really surprising.
Transparent heads?\nI kind of want to see that now.
Oh ho, it seems like you're becoming interested\nin the mysteries of the deep sea.
I also want to see it with you, Futaro.
Wha?\nWell, I mean, this is our graduation trip, so being\nwith you guys and Raiha is the best case, right?
Hmm...\nFutaro-kun, I don't think that's necessarily true.
Ooh...
Alright, let's all see it together.
Y-yeah.
